nodes.label: Orchestrator Role
nodes.rationale: The main session defines work, delegates, and triages; it never writes code or docs itself. Atomic tasks (one Linear issue, one PR, one agent, no mid-task decisions) keep briefs complete and reviews severity-triaged (HIGH fix immediately, MEDIUM flag human, LOW batch into cleanup ticket).

nodes.label: documentation/ Is the Source of Truth
nodes.rationale: Code must conform to documentation contracts (database-schema, api-reference, sync-protocol); contract changes update the doc in the same cycle so every client and the backend are checked against a single shared specification rather than another codebase's implementation.

nodes.label: Git Flow: One Ticket, One Branch, One PR
nodes.rationale: Never work on main; always branch feat/RIZ-<n>-<slug> from fresh origin/main; Conventional Commits referencing RIZ-<n>; PR titled [RIZ-<n>] merges into submodule main followed by a pointer-bump commit in the master repo. Keeps work atomic and traceable to Linear.

nodes.label: Containerization Requirement
nodes.rationale: Every containerizable project provides a Dockerfile and runs end-to-end with docker compose; rize-desktop and rize-mobile are exempt because macOS/iOS GUI apps cannot run in Linux containers.

nodes.label: Rize-Clone Git Flow (one ticket → one branch → one PR)
nodes.rationale: One Linear ticket RIZ-<n> maps to one branch feat/RIZ-<n>-<slug> and one PR [RIZ-<n>] <summary> with Conventional Commits, keeping every change atomic and traceable; never open a PR with failing tests or lint.

hyperedges.label: Orchestrator Delegation Workflow (brief, implement, review, document)
